name: Old Munich Sauerbraten (Clay-pot)
recipeIngredient: 5 lb Bottom round roast of beef
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all the ingredients for the marinade in a porcelain-enameled container.
2. Add the meat and marinate for at least 10 days, turning twice daily.
3. Before cooking, presoak a large clay pot, top and bottom, in water for 15 minutes.
4. Remove the meat from the marinade and sear on all sides in hot bacon fat in a frying pan.
5. In a saucepan, reduce the marinade to one-third its original volume by boiling rapidly, uncovered.
6. Place the meat in the presoaked pot, then add the reduced marinade and all other ingredients except arrowroot.
7. Cover the pot and place it in a cold oven set to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
8. Cook for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until nearly done.
9. Remove the pot from the oven and pour off the sauce.
10. Return the pot to the oven, uncovered, for an additional 10 minutes of cooking.
11. Blend the sauce in a container, tasting for seasoning. The sauce should be sweet and sour.
12. If needed, add brown sugar and/or vinegar, then thicken with arrowroot.
13. Serve the sauce over the meat and German potato pancakes.
